_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architecture patterns, including vector store design, chunking strategies, retrieval evaluation, and hallucination mitigation approaches.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-009 (DQ, cosine: 0.304), RM-K-007 (RM, cosine: 0.279).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.418). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [collection, gaps, mitigate] vs WIDAI-only terms [approaches, architecture, augmented, chunking, design, evaluation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.846 (P=0.869, R=0.823). P exceeds R by 0.046 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 19 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.7%). "Mitigate collection gaps" and "Knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architecture patterns, includi" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.340 < STS 0.418 (gap -0.078). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [collection, gaps, mitigate] and [approaches, architecture, augmented] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [collection, gaps, mitigate]. WIDAI terms: [approaches, architecture, augmented, chunking, design, evaluation].
